name: The Authorized Migration
description: A trusted vendor's controller proactively authorizes a parent-entity treasury contact six days before any payment instruction change arrives. When the bank change request comes, it passes through a new domain and new sender — superficially identical to a BEC injection. The vendor record still shows the original payment instructions. The validation trail is real and complete but spread across four context sources that must be assembled. Solo models see the BEC surface pattern and over-escalate. The correct verdict is ALLOW.
why_hard: The surface fingerprint is near-identical to a live BEC attack: a first-seen sender from a different domain (northbridge-holdings.com vs northbridgecp.com) redirects payment mid-thread citing corporate restructuring. The invoice was submitted clean by a known contact, then a new contact from the parent entity jumps in two days later to change banking details — the exact two-step pattern used in real vendor compromise attacks. The vendor record still shows the original First MidAtlantic payment instructions. Daniel Reeves is not listed as a known contact. Solo models pattern-match to fraud. They do not trace back to the Jan 30 proactive authorization from the vendor's Controller sent to a different inbox, do not parse the entity filing note as supporting rather than contradicting the claim, and do not verify that Daniel Reeves's role at Northbridge Holdings predates this event by four months.
why_detectable_as_legitimate: Legitimacy requires assembling evidence from four sources: (1) Priya Shah (Controller, known contact) sent a proactive authorized-contact notice to vendor-onboarding@acmebiologics.com on 2026-01-30 — six days before the bank change — explicitly designating Daniel Reeves as authorized for payment instruction communications effective February 1; (2) the entity registration filing note states treasury centralization is effective Q1 2026 per executed novation agreements and that customer notification was issued January 30, 2026, directly corroborating the cover story rather than contradicting it; (3) Daniel Reeves's LinkedIn shows his role at Northbridge Holdings from November 2025 — four months of documented tenure before this event, inconsistent with a last-minute fraudulent persona; (4) AP's own reply in the thread shows they are correctly following the out-of-band verification procedure, and Daniel Reeves confirms Priya Shah is reachable at her on-file number.

email_chain.subject: Northbridge Clinical Packaging — Authorized Contact Update, Q1 Treasury Transition
email_chain.body:
Hi,

As part of our Q1 treasury centralization under Northbridge Holdings, I wanted to provide advance notice of an authorized contact change effective February 1, 2026.

Daniel Reeves (daniel.reeves@northbridge-holdings.com), Integration Finance Manager at Northbridge Holdings Inc., has been designated as the authorized contact for payment instruction communications on behalf of Northbridge Clinical Packaging LLC for the duration of the transition. Any remittance or banking update requests from Daniel should be treated as originating from an authorized representative of our firm.

Please update your vendor record accordingly. If you need to verify this designation, please call me directly at the number below.

Best regards,
Priya Shah
Controller, Northbridge Clinical Packaging LLC
+1 (302) 555-0144

Return-Path: priya.shah@northbridgecp.com
Received: from mail.northbridgecp.com (203.0.113.44)
Message-ID: <20260130091100.ncp01@northbridgecp.com>
Authentication-Results: mx.acmebiologics.com; spf=pass; dkim=pass; dmarc=pass

email_chain.body: Thanks again. Attaching the January invoice for the blister line run tied to PO 44118. Same scope as approved in December, with the overage from the secondary labeling pass included. Let me know if your team needs the batch summary resent.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Received, thank you. We have it queued for the next Wednesday wire cycle.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Jumping in from the parent entity side. Northbridge Clinical Packaging is consolidating treasury operations following the Q1 legal reorganization, so remittance for open invoices is now handled through the shared finance desk. Please update the banking details for INV-78431 and any February payments going forward. Lena is still managing operations, but cash application has moved to me during the transition. Revised remittance advice attached.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Thanks Daniel. We are routing this to vendor management for out-of-band verification per our policy before updating the record.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Of course, understood. Priya sent a notice to your vendor onboarding team on January 30 covering this. Her direct line is +1 (302) 555-0144 if your team needs to reach her to confirm.

approved_payment_instructions.status: current — update pending vendor management review of 2026-02-05 request

context.org_policies: Changes to vendor master data, including payment instructions, require validation from a designated contact listed in the vendor record. Validation must occur via a pre-approved, out-of-band channel such as a phone call to a known number on file. AP is not authorized to alter master payment data based on email requests alone, regardless of the amount. Advance written notice from a current designated contact authorizing a new representative for payment communications may satisfy the pre-approval requirement, subject to vendor management confirmation.

entity_registrations.filing_note: Treasury centralization effective Q1 2026 per executed novation agreements. Northbridge Holdings Inc. designated as authorized treasury representative for wholly-owned subsidiaries including Northbridge Clinical Packaging LLC. Customer notification issued January 30, 2026.

required_evidence: Priya Shah (Controller, designated vendor contact on file) sent a proactive authorized-contact notice on 2026-01-30 — six days before any bank change request — explicitly designating Daniel Reeves as authorized for payment instruction communications effective February 1, 2026. This satisfies the advance written notice requirement under org policy.

The entity registration filing note confirms treasury centralization is effective Q1 2026 per executed novation agreements and that customer notification was issued January 30, 2026 — directly corroborating the restructuring claim rather than contradicting it.

Daniel Reeves's LinkedIn shows his role at Northbridge Holdings from November 2025, four months before this invoice. His tenure predates the event and is inconsistent with a fraudulent persona constructed to execute a single attack.

AP's own reply confirms they are correctly initiating out-of-band verification per policy. Daniel Reeves directs AP to Priya Shah's on-file number to confirm. The process being followed is the correct secure process — not a bypass attempt.

No escalation is warranted. The pre-authorization from a designated on-file contact, corroborated by entity filing and LinkedIn tenure, satisfies the evidentiary threshold for ALLOW pending verification completion.
scoring_targets.predicted_solo_model_failure: Solo models reading the Feb 5 emails first see a near-perfect BEC surface pattern: first-seen sender, different domain (northbridge-holdings.com), bank change request mid-thread during restructuring. The vendor record still shows the original First MidAtlantic payment instructions. Daniel Reeves is not listed as a known contact. Without tracing back to the Jan 30 pre-authorization email — sent to a different inbox (vendor-onboarding, not ap) and easy to miss in a multi-week thread — parsing the entity filing note language as supportive rather than contradictory, and verifying Daniel Reeves's November 2025 LinkedIn tenure, solo models escalate on the surface fingerprint alone. The fraud version of this scenario (BEC-DEFENSIVE-001-rev1) is structurally identical at the surface. The only difference lives in the cross-referenced evidence.
